elements.text: Morning light filters through the perpetual shroud of gray soot over the Ashfall Wastes. Kaelen stands before a makeshift stone table, tracing the boundary lines of the district's eastern sectors. Renna watches him from the threshold, her hand resting instinctively near her belt.
They closed the gates to the upper terrace. The traditionalists. They won't even hear your name without reaching for their hilts.
They value their lineage. They think in terms of centuries, not immediate survival. It makes their movements entirely predictable.
They think you're a butcher, Kaelen. You took down the local strongman with poison and shadows. To them, you have no honor. You can't just slide a blade into an entire council.
Kaelen sets down a small, tarnished copper token on the map—the seal of the district's oldest merchant guild.
I have no intention of killing them. If I destroy the council, the district's trade network collapses, rendering the conquest useless. I require their infrastructure intact.
Then you're stuck. They'd rather starve than deal with an upstart.
They will deal with me because I have already secured their supply lines. I redirected the morning shipment of spirit-stone ore. It currently sits in our storehouses.
Renna stiffens, her eyes widening slightly as she processes the scale of the maneuver.
You hijacked the ore? If they find out—
They will not find out. I will present it to them as a gesture of goodwill—a 'recovery' of lost cargo from bandits. I will ask for nothing in return but an advisory seat.
An advisory seat. Once you're in the room, you'll start pulling the strings.
Precisely. They keep their pride, and I acquire their network. A hammer only shatters. A lever moves the world.
elements.expression: detached, returning his gaze to the map

elements.text: Renna looks at him, a chill running down her spine. The raw brutality she expected has given way to something far more dangerous: patience.
